name: Haleiwa
desc: Legendary surf town — plantation charm, food trucks, shave ice, art galleries, no buildings over 2 stories.
insider: Matsumoto's shave ice is famous but Aoki's across the road is just as good with half the line
The surf breaks right in Haleiwa Harbor — watch the boats navigate around surfers in winter swells
Thursday evening art walk in summer is when the town really comes alive with gallery openings and live music
The historic Rainbow Bridge at the town entrance is the iconic photo spot — go at golden hour for the best light
Haleiwa Beach Park on the harbor side has calmer water than the open-ocean surf breaks — good for families with small kids
Coffee Gallery in the North Shore Marketplace makes the best espresso on the North Shore — locals swear by it
No building in town is over two stories — a county ordinance keeps the old plantation town character intact
Parking in town is free but the main lot fills up by 10am on weekends — park along Kamehameha Highway and walk in
The Thursday afternoon farmers market near Waimea Valley has local produce, honey, and prepared foods at real prices
Eddie Aikau, legendary big-wave surfer and lifeguard, is honored here — Haleiwa celebrates him deeply every winter
Winter brings 30-foot surf and the Vans Triple Crown contest series — the town population triples November through February
